topTargets.name: Carnell Tate
topTargets.reasoning: The 1.01 lock. RSP DoT 92.3 (highest in entire 2026 class at any position), Franchise tier with a Randy Moss comp, and consensus ADP 9.46 confirms he is universally the 1.01-or-1.02. With $11.4M cap space and medium RSP affinity, FIRE has zero reason to overthink this — RSP and consensus agree.

topTargets.name: Jeremiyah Love
topTargets.reasoning: If Tate goes 1.01, Love is the only realistic 1.01 alternative — RSP #2 (DoT 89.3, All-Pro ceiling, Kamara comp) and consensus ADP 3.59 (the highest-ranked rookie on MFL ADP). FIRE's RB room is just 5 deep and needs a workhorse anchor; medium RSP affinity means consensus ADP 3.59 sways them.

topTargets.name: Fernando Mendoza
topTargets.reasoning: Logical 1.14 target. RSP QB1 at #7 overall (DoT 85.6, 'one of the best' downfield creators) and consensus ADP 11.39 puts him squarely in 1.14 range. With only 2 QBs rostered and 3 contracts expiring, a franchise QB on a rookie deal is high-leverage roster work.

topTargets.name: Jadarian Price
topTargets.reasoning: 2.01 sweet spot. RSP #21 (DoT 83.5, 'ceiling as high as RBs 2-5') with consensus ADP 16.05 making him a turn-of-round 2 value. Doubles down on the RB-thin room behind a potential Love selection or stands alone if Tate went 1.01.

topTargets.name: Eli Stowers
topTargets.reasoning: 3.01 swing. RSP #11 (DoT 84.4, 'classic move TE, on cusp of Starter tier') but consensus ADP 21.25 suggests he slides into round 3. Even with 5 TEs already rostered, 3 contracts expiring means churn is coming, and Stowers is a clear value gap pick at 3.01.

capPosture: Healthy — $11.4M of $45M cap space with no taxi/IR usage, easily absorbs all five rookie contracts; can active-roster the top-2 picks and taxi the rest.

wildcard.name: Skyler Bell
wildcard.reasoning: RSP #6 (DoT 85.9, Ja'Marr Chase high-end comp) but completely absent from MFL dynasty ADP top 30 — a classic medium-affinity blend pick at 2.16 where the RSP gap over consensus is largest. FIRE has 10 WRs already so they can afford a high-variance dart.

summary: FIRE is sitting on a premium hand — 1.01, 1.14, 2.01, 2.16, 3.01 — with healthy cap and a roster that needs RB depth and a QB-of-the-future more than another WR. Expected play: take Tate at 1.01 (RSP and consensus both scream it), grab Mendoza or the best RB at 1.14, then double-dip on RB/TE value at 2.01 and 3.01. Wildcard at 2.16 is where their medium RSP affinity shows up — a Skyler Bell-type RSP-darling, consensus-fader.
